name: Indonesia
overview: Indonesia's Personal Data Protection Law (Law No. 27 of 2022, 'PDP Law') is a GDPR-inspired comprehensive statute whose two-year transition period ended in October 2024, making it fully applicable. Implementing regulations and the dedicated supervisory institution mandated by the law have lagged; oversight currently sits with the Ministry of Communication and Digital Affairs (Komdigi).

regulator.name: Ministry of Communication and Digital Affairs (Komdigi) — pending dedicated PDP institution

legal_instruments.title: Law No. 27 of 2022 on Personal Data Protection (PDP Law)
legal_instruments.issuing_authority: House of Representatives (DPR) / President of Indonesia
legal_instruments.summary: Comprehensive GDPR-style law: lawful bases, general and specific (sensitive) data categories, data subject rights, DPO requirement for certain controllers, 3x24-hour breach notification, and administrative fines up to 2% of annual revenue plus criminal sanctions.

legal_instruments.title: Government Regulation No. 71 of 2019 on Electronic Systems and Transactions (GR 71)
legal_instruments.summary: Pre-PDP framework for electronic system operators, including registration, data handling and localisation rules for public-sector systems; continues to apply alongside the PDP Law.

developments.title: PDP Law transition period ends — law fully applicable
developments.summary: The two-year grace period expired, making all controller/processor obligations enforceable, though the implementing regulation and the supervisory institution remained pending.

developments.title: Draft implementing regulation (RPP PDP) progresses
developments.summary: The government continued finalising the PDP Law's implementing government regulation and the design of the data protection authority under presidential oversight.

penalties: Administrative fines up to 2% of annual revenue/receipts, plus corrective orders and publication of violations; criminal penalties up to 6 years' imprisonment and IDR 6 billion for unlawful disclosure or falsification.
cross_border: Transfers permitted if the destination has equal-or-higher protection, adequate binding safeguards exist, or the data subject consents.
